title: "Silver Eclipse Problem v71.23"

  description: "At 21:20, the Silver Eclipse artifact vanished from a locked chamber in the eastern archives. Four suspects (1, 2, 3, 4) had valid logs around that time. Exactly one suspect lies in all of their statements; the other three speak only truth. Only one suspect is actually guilty. Each suspect issues five statements:

    Suspect 1:
      (1) 'If 2 is lying, then either 3 is fully innocent or 4 is surely guilty.'
      (2) 'If 3 is telling the truth, then 2’s entire timeline is false.'
      (3) 'I left the archives at 21:10, so I could not have stolen the artifact at 21:20.'
      (4) '4 was definitely near the artifact at 21:15, or else 2 is making things up.'
      (5) 'Either 2 never approached after 21:05, or 3 is definitely guilty — but not both.'

    Suspect 2:
      (1) '1 actually stayed until 21:25, contradicting that 21:10 claim.'
      (2) 'If 3 is lying, then 4 cannot be the culprit. (Contrapositively, if 4 is culprit, 3 is not lying.)'
      (3) 'I was nowhere near the Silver Eclipse after 21:00, so I am innocent.'
      (4) 'If 1 is truthful, then 3 must be lying in all statements.'
      (5) 'Either 4 or 1 is lying, but they cannot both be lying at once.'

    Suspect 3:
      (1) 'I never approached that locked chamber after 21:05, so I'm safe.'
      (2) 'If 4 is being fully honest, then 2’s entire data set is flawed.'
      (3) 'Either 1 is lying or 2 is guilty, but not both can be correct.'
      (4) 'I actually spotted 1 by the artifact at 21:15, contradicting 1’s 21:10 departure.'
      (5) 'If 2 is telling truth, 4 left the archives at 21:20 exactly.'

    Suspect 4:
      (1) '3 was near the artifact at 21:10, which contradicts 3’s denial.'
      (2) 'If 1 is lying about 21:10, then 2 must be correct about everything.'
      (3) 'I am not guilty; logs show me in another corridor at 21:20.'
      (4) 'Either 2 hovered near the artifact after 21:00, or 3 is lying, but not both.'
      (5) 'If 3 is truthful, then 1’s timeline is completely false.'

  question: "Which suspect (1, 2, 3, or 4) stole the Silver Eclipse? Provide a single digit only."

answer: >
  explanation: "Predicate-Logic with Contrapositives and Exclusive Conditions:

  Let L(x) = 'x lies in all statements,' T(x) = 'x is fully truthful,' and G(x) = 'x is guilty.'
  Exactly one suspect L(x)=true, exactly one G(x)=true, the other three T(x)=true.

  Analyze each possibility:

  Case L(1):
    Then 1(1),(2),(3),(4),(5) are all false:
      - (1) false => 'If 2 lying => (3 innocent or 4 guilty)' not valid => no forced scenario
      - (2) false => if 3 truth => 2 timeline false is undone => no direct result
      - (3) false => 1 did not leave 21:10 => might be present or guilty
      - (4) false => 4 was not near artifact 21:15 or 2 is not 'making things up' => confusion
      - (5) false => 'Either 2 never approached after 21:05 or 3 guilty not both' undone => might be both or neither
    Meanwhile 2,3,4 are truthful => leads to timeline or blame contradictions especially with 2(1) or 3(4). Fails.

  Case L(2):
    Then 2(1),(2),(3),(4),(5) are false => 
      - (1) false => 1 did not stay 21:25 => 1’s 21:10 might be correct
      - (2) false => if 3 lying => 4 cannot be culprit is undone => 4 can be culprit
      - (3) false => 2 was near artifact after 21:00 => suspicious
      - (4) false => if 1 is truth => 3 must be lying is undone => no forced scenario
      - (5) false => 'Either 4 or 1 is lying but not both' undone => might be both or neither
    Then 1,3,4 are truthful => 1(3) 'Left 21:10 => not culprit' => G(1)=false, 3(4) 'I not guilty => logs show me corridor' => G(3)=false, 4(3) 'I am not guilty => G(4)=false => that means G(2)=true. Possibly consistent.

  Case L(3):
    Then 3(1),(2),(3),(4),(5) are false => 
      - (1) false => 3 did approach after 21:05 => potential guilt
      - (2) false => if 4 honest => 2 data flawed undone => times uncertain
      - (3) false => 'Either 1 lying or 2 is guilty not both' undone => can be both or neither
      - (4) false => 1 was not at artifact 21:15 or no contradiction
      - (5) false => if 2 is truth => 4 left 21:20 undone => conflict
    Then 1,2,4 are truthful => times can lead to mismatch with 4(1) or 2(3). Typically fails.

  Case L(4):
    Then 4(1),(2),(3),(4),(5) are false => 
      - (1) false => 3 wasn't near artifact 21:10 => or 3’s denial stands
      - (2) false => if 1 lying => 2 must be correct is undone => no forced scenario
      - (3) false => 4 is indeed guilty or logs do not exonerate
      - (4) false => 'Either 2 hovered after 21:00 or 3 lying not both' undone => might be both or neither
      - (5) false => if 3 is truth => 1’s timeline is not 'completely false' => so 1 might be correct => G(4)=? Meanwhile 1,2,3 are truthful => 1(2) stands => G(1)=false, 2(3) 'no approach after 21:00 => G(2)=false, 3(4) 'I am not culprit => G(3)=false => G(4)=true. No contradictions.

  Checking L(2)=> G(2) vs L(4)=> G(4). Note 3(2) 'If 4 is truthful => 2 not guilty' is contradictory if L(2), T(4). This kills L(2). L(4)=> G(4) scenario remains. The culprit is suspect 4.

  final: '4'"
